rulings.Case Title: IN RE: 1330 VALENCIA STREET, SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94110
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING RE: SURPLUS FUNDS FROM PROCEEDS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 23, 2024 line 1. HEARING RE: SURPLUS FUNDS FROM PROCEEDS Hearing Required. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHN E. WAI VS. FATHI SAID ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motio For Instructions Regarding Allocation Of Rents
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 23, 2024 line 3. RECEIVER KEVIN SINGER'S MOTION FOR INSTRUCTIONS REGARDING ALLOCATION OF RENTS is GRANTED. Receiver is instructed to proceed with the payment of expenses without any changes. The issues arising out of the 30 and 120-day notices are not formally before this Court in this action via complaint, cross-complaint or answer. The parties do not cite any authority for the proposition that the determination of rent amount under the circumstances of this case may be property addressed on receiver's motion for instructions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WILMINGTON SAVINGS FUND SOCIETY, FSB, VS. LUCIA ANNA PAULAZZO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 23, 2024 line 4. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Plaintiff seeks a summary judgment as to Lucia Anna Paulazzo and Bruno Paulazzo. Motion is denied as to Lucia Anna. No answer on file by this defendant. CCP 1170.7. Motion is DENIED as to Bruno, Plaintiff provides no argument (See Points and Authorities) or evidence (See separate statement) showing that Plaintiff is entitled to recover possession from this defendant. The Court notes that while there is no response by Lucia Anna on file, there is also no default. The Court further notes that while Plaintiff mentions in passaging that Bruno's answer is "inappropriate" this comment is not supported by authority or argument. The Court further notes that there is no explanation in the Points and Authorities or in the Request for Judicial Notice for the assertions made in paragraph 9 of the Request for Judicial Notice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ERP OPERATING, LP VS. ADAM CHARLES BARNETT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Plaintiff; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 23, 2024 line 5. GEARY COURTYARD 639 LLC'S MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE PLAINTIFF is DENIED without prejudice. The moving party seeks to be substituted in as a Plaintiff as the new owner of the property. However, the original Plaintiff did not bring this action based on their ownership interest in the property. Form Complaint in paragraph 4 provides a check box for "as owner;" however Plaintiff chose to forgo that express option and chose "other (specify): landlord." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
